headline: Oracle confirmation cycle. Pre-litigation status unchanged: WARN Act investigations remain active in Washington and Missouri (Strauss Borrelli plus additional firms), no lawsuits yet filed. May 30 statutory window opens. No new entity-specific structural events in window. Resolves pending 2026-04-30 proposal with confirmation. Scores held.
key_evidence: WARN Act investigations active in WA and MO (Strauss Borrelli PLLC plus additional firms beyond initial filing).
No lawsuits filed yet within window — pre-litigation status sustained.
Oracle filed WARN notices in at least six states on March 31 (same-day termination date) — 60-day notice not provided.
Statutory argument anticipated: Oracle may claim remote workers are not at 'single site' under WARN Act; circuit-split exists.
May 30 2026: WARN Act 60-day window opens — primary monitor trigger.
No new positive compliance disclosures found in window.

evidence.finding: Oracle Washington WARN Act investigation filed April 2026 — initial process step in pre-litigation phase.
Oracle WARN notices filed in at least six states on March 31; same-day terminations imply notice was not provided 60 days prior.

scoring_rationale.EMP: 1.8 unchanged. Pre-litigation status — outcome will be scored when material.

math_check.validator_check: Zero delta; minor rounding drift from canonical formula consistent with prior cycle.

negativeSignals: WARN Act investigations active in two states; same-day-termination filing pattern; no positive compliance disclosure.
sector_context: Across Fortune 500 tech sector AI-attributed layoff narrative, Oracle is in pre-litigation status while Meta and Microsoft are post-execution. May 30 marks Oracle's transition from pre-litigation to active-litigation if filings occur.
open_actions_carried: Schedule mandatory monitor flag for May 30 WARN Act litigation window
If lawsuits filed by May 30, full reassessment in next cycle
